perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information . “Confidential Information ” means all the information of the Disclosing Party furnish ed or made available , orally, in writing, by electronic means, or by inspection, at any time on and after the Effective Date regarding the Disclosing Party or its business plans , processes, technology, data, material, documents regarding the infrastructure, businesses, assets, liabilities, financial conditions, operations, identity of personnel, inte llectual property (including, without limitation, trade secrets), Personal Data (defined below ) and other related or its similar information provided in all cases such information is marked conspicuously with a confidentiality or a substantially similar le gend in accordance with Section 4 of this Agreement .
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information . “Confidential Information ” means some of the information of the Disclosing Party furnished or made available , orally, in writing, by electronic means, or by inspection, at any time on and after the Effective Date regarding the Disclosing Party or its business plans , processes, technology, data, material, documents regarding the infrastructure, businesses, assets, liabilities, financial conditions, operations, identity of personnel, intellectual property and other related or its similar information provided in all cases such information is marked conspicuously with a confidentiality or a substantially similar legend in accordance with Section 4 of this Agreement .
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'all the information' to 'some of the information', the definition of 'Confidential Information' becomes ambiguous. It is no longer clear which information is covered by the agreement, contradicting Section 3 which requires the Recipient to protect 'Confidential Information'. The 'some of the information' change creates an in-text contradiction when referring to Section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Duty . The Recipient shall not disclose the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to any third party nor access or use such Confi dential Information other than solely for the Purpose . In all cases, t he Recipient shall use reasonable measures that are at least as stringent as those used by the Recipient to protect its own information of a similar nature (and in no event shall the Re cipient use less than reasonable measures) to keep confidential and safeguard from theft loss and/or unpermitted uses the Confidential Information disclosed to Recipient. In addition, t he Recipient shall limit access to Confidential Information to those o fficers, directors, employees and associated researchers within the Recipient 's organization who reasonably require access to the Confidential Information for carrying out the Purpose and who have agreed to maintain the Confidential Information in confiden ce. The fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning the P urpose , including without limitation the status or termination thereof, and any terms, conditions or other facts with respect to the P urpose (including, but not limited to, the existence and terms of this Agreement) are Confidential Information subject to the confidentiality requirements set forth herein. Recipient shall implement and maintain appropriate security measures for Personal Data which shall be at least as protective of the confidentiality of such information as the safeguards for personal information set forth under applicable federal, state and local law .
perturbation.justification: The change from "all the information" to "some of the information" in the definition of Confidential Information introduces ambiguity. The Contradicted Text, Section 3, states that 'The Recipient shall not disclose the Confidential Information', but if 'Confidential Information' is now 'some of the information', it's unclear which information is covered and should be protected, creating a contradiction. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' type as essential language defining the scope of confidential information has been altered, impacting obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “Personal Data” shall mean any and all personally identifiable information, in any form or media, about current or former NU faculty members, employees, students, prospec tive students, and other persons associated with NU. Personal Data includes, without limitation, (i) an individual’s social security number, bank or other financial account numbers, credit or debit card numbers, driver’s license number, passport number, o ther government -issued identification numbers, biometric data, health and medical information, and data about the individual obtained through a Project; (ii) financial information, employee benefits information, education records, NU identification numbers ; Mutual Non -Disclosure Agre ement Northeastern University and (iii) any additional categories of information about individuals that NU from time to time designates in writing as Personal Data.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “Personal Data” shall mean some personally identifiable information, in any form or media, about current or former NU faculty members, employees, students, prospec tive students, and other persons associated with NU. Personal Data includes, without limitation, (i) an individual’s social security number, bank or other financial account numbers, credit or debit card numbers, driver’s license number, passport number, o ther government -issued identification numbers, biometric data, health and medical information, and data about the individual obtained through a Project; (ii) financial information, employee benefits information, education records, NU identification numbers ; Mutual Non -Disclosure Agre ement Northeastern University and (iii) any additional categories of information about individuals that NU from time to time designates in writing as Personal Data.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'any and all personally identifiable information' to 'some personally identifiable information' creates uncertainty. It is unclear which Personal Data is covered, contradicting the obligations in Section 3 to protect Personal Data. This uncertainty directly impacts the meaning and enforceability of Section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Duty . The Recipient shall not disclose the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to any third party nor access or use such Confi dential Information other than solely for the Purpose . In all cases, t he Recipient shall use reasonable measures that are at least as stringent as those used by the Recipient to protect its own information of a similar nature (and in no event shall the Re cipient use less than reasonable measures) to keep confidential and safeguard from theft loss and/or unpermitted uses the Confidential Information disclosed to Recipient. In addition, t he Recipient shall limit access to Confidential Information to those o fficers, directors, employees and associated researchers within the Recipient 's organization who reasonably require access to the Confidential Information for carrying out the Purpose and who have agreed to maintain the Confidential Information in confiden ce. The fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning the P urpose , including without limitation the status or termination thereof, and any terms, conditions or other facts with respect to the P urpose (including, but not limited to, the existence and terms of this Agreement) are Confidential Information subject to the confidentiality requirements set forth herein. Recipient shall implement and maintain appropriate security measures for Personal Data which shall be at least as protective of the confidentiality of such information as the safeguards for personal information set forth under applicable federal, state and local law .
perturbation.justification: The original text defines "Personal Data" as "any and all personally identifiable information." The changed text modifies this to "some personally identifiable information." This creates uncertainty regarding which specific personal data is covered under the agreement. The contradicted text, Section 3 (Duty), states that the Recipient shall implement and maintain appropriate security measures for Personal Data, but because of the change, it's unclear which 'some' personal data this applies to. Therefore, the change creates a direct contradiction because it undermines the Recipient's obligation to protect 'Personal Data' as it makes the scope of 'Personal Data' uncertain, creating ambiguity and impacting the enforceability of Section 3.

perturbation.original_text: Exclusions. Notwithstanding the foregoing, “Confidential Information” shall not include the following types of informa tion if the Recipient establishes by competent proof that the information : (i) is now or subsequently becomes generally available to the public without a breach of this Agreement by the Recipient ; (ii) was known to or rightfully in the possession of the Recipient p rior to the date of disclosure of such Confidential Information ; (iii) is developed by the Recipient independent of the disclosure made under this Agreement ; (iv) is supplied to the Recipient by a third party without restriction on such disclosure ; or (v) is required to be disclosed pursuant to a valid subpoena, court order or other requirement(s) of applicable law , provided, however, that the foregoing exceptions to Confidential Information shall not apply to Personal Data .
perturbation.changed_text: Exclusions. Notwithstanding the foregoing, “Confidential Information” shall not include the following types of informa tion if the Recipient establishes by proof that the information : (i) is now or subsequently becomes generally available to the public without a breach of this Agreement by the Recipient ; (ii) was known to or rightfully in the possession of the Recipient p rior to the date of disclosure of such Confidential Information ; (iii) is developed by the Recipient independent of the disclosure made under this Agreement ; (iv) is supplied to the Recipient by a third party without restriction on such disclosure ; or (v) is required to be disclosed pursuant to a valid subpoena, court order or other requirement(s) of applicable law , provided, however, that the foregoing exceptions to Confidential Information shall not apply to Personal Data .
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'competent' from 'competent proof', the standard of proof required to establish an exclusion to Confidential Information is lowered. This creates an in-text contradiction because Section 3 specifies the Recipient must use 'reasonable measures' to protect Confidential Information; lowering the standard of proof makes it easier for the Recipient to claim information is excluded, weakening the 'reasonable measures' protection requirement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Duty . The Recipient shall not disclose the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to any third party nor access or use such Confi dential Information other than solely for the Purpose . In all cases, t he Recipient shall use reasonable measures that are at least as stringent as those used by the Recipient to protect its own information of a similar nature (and in no event shall the Re cipient use less than reasonable measures) to keep confidential and safeguard from theft loss and/or unpermitted uses the Confidential Information disclosed to Recipient. In addition, t he Recipient shall limit access to Confidential Information to those o fficers, directors, employees and associated researchers within the Recipient 's organization who reasonably require access to the Confidential Information for carrying out the Purpose and who have agreed to maintain the Confidential Information in confiden ce. The fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning the P urpose , including without limitation the status or termination thereof, and any terms, conditions or other facts with respect to the P urpose (including, but not limited to, the existence and terms of this Agreement) are Confidential Information subject to the confidentiality requirements set forth herein. Recipient shall implement and maintain appropriate security measures for Personal Data which shall be at least as protective of the confidentiality of such information as the safeguards for personal information set forth under applicable federal, state and local law .
perturbation.justification: The change removes 'competent' from 'competent proof', lowering the standard of evidence required to claim an exclusion to 'Confidential Information'. This directly contradicts the 'Duty' section, which requires the Recipient to use 'reasonable measures' to protect Confidential Information. By making it easier to claim an exclusion, the change weakens the 'reasonable measures' protection requirement, leading to a clear conflict and inconsistency within the document, based on the explanation that was provided.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Duty . The Recipient shall not disclose the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to any third party nor access or use such Confi dential Information other than solely for the Purpose . In all cases, t he Recipient shall use reasonable measures that are at least as stringent as those used by the Recipient to protect its own information of a similar nature (and in no event shall the Re cipient use less than reasonable measures) to keep confidential and safeguard from theft loss and/or unpermitted uses the Confidential Information disclosed to Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Duty . The Recipient shall not disclose the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to any third party nor access or use such Confi dential Information other than solely for the Purpose . In all cases, t he Recipient shall use some measures that are at least as stringent as those used by the Recipient to protect its own information of a similar nature to keep confidential and safeguard from theft loss and/or unpermitted uses the Confidential Information disclosed to Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'reasonable measures' to 'some measures' reduces the required level of protection for Confidential Information. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 11, which disclaims warranties regarding the accuracy of the information and limits liability. By reducing the required protection, the disclaimer of liability becomes more problematic, as the Disclosing Party has less assurance that their information will be adequately protected, given the changes on the Duty section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Representations & Warranties . Each Party represents and warrants that (i) i t has the authority to enter into this Agreement, (ii) it is not a party to any agreement with any third party which prohibits it from fulfilling its obligations under this Agreement and (iii) this Agreement, when executed Mutual Non -Disclosure Agre ement Northeastern University and delivered, shall be a valid a nd binding obligation on such Party, enforceable in accordance in its terms. , The disclosure of Confidential Information shall not constitute any representation, warranty, assurance, or guarantee, with respect to non -infringement of any patent or other pr oprietary rights of others. NOTWITHSTANDING ANY PROVISION IN THIS AGREEMENT , NEITHER PARTY MAKES ANY REPRESENTATIONS OR WARRANTIES AS TO THE COMPLETENESS, ACCURACY, MERCHANTABILITY OR FITNESS FOR USE FOR ANY PURPOSE OF THE INFORMATION DISCLOSED UNDER THIS AGREEMENT, WHICH IS DELIVERED “AS IS” . EXCEPT AS SET FORTH IN SECTION 7 OF THIS AGREEMENT AND IN THE CASES OF FRAUD OR WILLFUL MISCONDUCT, THE DISCLOSING PARTY SHALL HAVE NO LIABILITY FOR ANY DIRECT OR INDIRECT DAMAGES WHICH MAY ARISE FROM THE RECIPIENT’S POSSESSION OR USE OF SUCH INFORMATION.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'reasonable measures' to 'some measures' in Section 3 weakens the protection required for Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the 'AS IS' disclaimer and limitation of liability in the Contradicted Text. By reducing the level of protection, the Disclosing Party bears a greater risk that their information will be compromised, directly conflicting with the intent of the disclaimer and limitation of liability. The disclaimer says the information is provided 'AS IS' and limits liability, but the change in Section 3 increases the potential for damages, creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Identification of Confidential Information . Confidential Information disclosed hereunder shall be disclosed i n written, electronic or other permanent form and shall be prominently identified as confidential using an appropriate legend, marking stamp, or other clear and conspicuous written identification which unambiguously indicates that the information being pro vided is the Disclosing Party 's Confidential Information. If Confidential Information is disclosed in other than written or other permanent form, it shall be considered Confidential Information as of the time of original disclosure if: i) the Disclosing Pa rty identifies it as Confidential Information at the time of the original disclosure and ii) Mutual Non -Disclosure Agre ement Northeastern University within thirty (30) days of the non -written disclosure, the Disclosing Party reduces the information to written or other permanent form, clearly identifying the inf ormation confidential as indicated above, and transmits it to the Recipient , referencing the place and date of such oral disclosure and identifying the name(s) of the employees or officers of the Recipient to whom it was made . The rights and obligations provided by this Agreement shall take precedence over specific legends or statements associated with Confidential Information when received .
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Identification of Confidential Information . Confidential Information disclosed hereunder shall be disclosed i n written, electronic or other permanent form and shall be prominently identified as confidential using an appropriate legend, marking stamp, or other clear and conspicuous written identification which unambiguously indicates that the information being pro vided is the Disclosing Party 's Confidential Information. If Confidential Information is disclosed in other than written or other permanent form, it shall be considered Confidential Information as of the time of original disclosure if: i) the Disclosing Pa rty identifies it as Confidential Information at the time of the original disclosure and ii) Mutual Non -Disclosure Agre ement Northeastern University within one-hundred-and-eighty (180) days of the non -written disclosure, the Disclosing Party reduces the information to written or other permanent form, clearly identifying the inf ormation confidential as indicated above, and transmits it to the Recipient , referencing the place and date of such oral disclosure and identifying the name(s) of the employees or officers of the Recipient to whom it was made . The rights and obligations provided by this Agreement shall take precedence over specific legends or statements associated with Confidential Information when received .
perturbation.explanation: Extending the timeframe from thirty (30) days to one-hundred-and-eighty (180) days, for the Disclosing Party to reduce the non-written disclosure to written form, contradicts Section 3 which states that ‘the Recipient shall use reasonable measures that are at least as stringent as those used by the Recipient to protect its own information of a similar nature’ and thus increasing the timeframe of writing the non-written disclosure to written form reduces the reasonable measures to protect the Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Duty . The Recipient shall not disclose the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to any third party nor access or use such Confi dential Information other than solely for the Purpose . In all cases, t he Recipient shall use reasonable measures that are at least as stringent as those used by the Recipient to protect its own information of a similar nature (and in no event shall the Re cipient use less than reasonable measures) to keep confidential and safeguard from theft loss and/or unpermitted uses the Confidential Information disclosed to Recipient. In addition, t he Recipient shall limit access to Confidential Information to those o fficers, directors, employees and associated researchers within the Recipient 's organization who reasonably require access to the Confidential Information for carrying out the Purpose and who have agreed to maintain the Confidential Information in confiden ce. The fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning the P urpose , including without limitation the status or termination thereof, and any terms, conditions or other facts with respect to the P urpose (including, but not limited to, the existence and terms of this Agreement) are Confidential Information subject to the confidentiality requirements set forth herein. Recipient shall implement and maintain appropriate security measures for Personal Data which shall be at least as protective of the confidentiality of such information as the safeguards for personal information set forth under applicable federal, state and local law .
perturbation.justification: The changed text extends the timeframe for reducing non-written disclosures to written form from thirty (30) days to one-hundred-and-eighty (180) days. This contradicts the contradicted text, which requires the Recipient to use 'reasonable measures that are at least as stringent as those used by the Recipient to protect its own information of a similar nature' to safeguard Confidential Information. By allowing a significantly longer period to formalize disclosures, the changed text reduces the protective measures for confidential information, creating a direct conflict with the requirement for 'reasonable measures' as defined in the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: 13. Term ; Non -disclosure obligation . The term of this Agreement shall be one ( 1) year from the Effective Date. This Agreement may be terminated by either pa rty upon thirty (30) days prior written notice to the other party. Termination or expiration of this Agreement shall not relieve either party of its obligations hereunder to maintain in confidence and not to use Confidential Information received hereunder for a period of three (3) years from the date of receipt thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 13. Term ; Non -disclosure obligation . The term of this Agreement shall be one ( 1) year from the Effective Date. This Agreement may be terminated by either pa rty upon thirty (30) days prior written notice to the other party. Termination or expiration of this Agreement shall not relieve either party of its obligations hereunder to maintain in confidence and not to use Confidential Information received hereunder for a period of one (1) year from the date of receipt thereof.
perturbation.explanation: By reducing the non-disclosure obligation timeframe from three (3) years to one (1) year after the expiration or termination of the agreement, creates an in-text contradiction in Section 3, which states that ‘the Recipient shall use reasonable measures that are at least as stringent as those used by the Recipient to protect its own information of a similar nature’ and thus reducing the non-disclosure timeframe limits the reasonable measures to protect the Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Duty . The Recipient shall not disclose the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to any third party nor access or use such Confi dential Information other than solely for the Purpose . In all cases, t he Recipient shall use reasonable measures that are at least as stringent as those used by the Recipient to protect its own information of a similar nature (and in no event shall the Re cipient use less than reasonable measures) to keep confidential and safeguard from theft loss and/or unpermitted uses the Confidential Information disclosed to Recipient. In addition, t he Recipient shall limit access to Confidential Information to those o fficers, directors, employees and associated researchers within the Recipient 's organization who reasonably require access to the Confidential Information for carrying out the Purpose and who have agreed to maintain the Confidential Information in confiden ce. The fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning the P urpose , including without limitation the status or termination thereof, and any terms, conditions or other facts with respect to the P urpose (including, but not limited to, the existence and terms of this Agreement) are Confidential Information subject to the confidentiality requirements set forth herein. Recipient shall implement and maintain appropriate security measures for Personal Data which shall be at least as protective of the confidentiality of such information as the safeguards for personal information set forth under applicable federal, state and local law .
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the non-disclosure obligation timeframe from three (3) years to one (1) year. The contradicted text requires the Recipient to use reasonable measures, at least as stringent as those used to protect their own information. By reducing the non-disclosure timeframe, the changed text limits the reasonable measures to protect the Confidential Information, creating a direct conflict with the requirement for measures 'at least as stringent' in the contradicted text. This fits the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' because essential language is altered, creating a contradiction with obligations stated in another section.
